title: After-Action Report — Make the almanac responsive on mobile
scope: On a phone the almanac scrolled sideways: the nav bar ran off both edges and the schedule table's columns were clipped. The cause was a single non-wrapping flex row in the header — there were zero media queries in `o27/almanac/static/almanac.css`. I added a `@media (max-width: 760px)` block (plus a `420px` refinement) that lets the header wrap and turns the nav into a self-contained horizontal-scroll strip, so the page width now stays pinned to the viewport. CSS-only change; no templates or Python touched.

rationale.item: `.site-bar` is `display:flex` with `gap:16px` and no wrap; it holds the brand, ten uppercase nav links, a 180px search box and a theme toggle — far wider than a ~390px phone, stretching `<body>` wider than the viewport.

The data tables were not the root cause: they already live inside `.tablewrap { overflow:auto }`; they only looked broken because the entire page was shifted by the header overflow. `_base.html.j2` already ships the correct viewport meta.

actions.description: Appended a responsive block to the end of almanac.css: @media (max-width: 760px) — .site-bar flex-wrap, brand on row one, search + toggle row two, nav last row with order:1; nav.top becomes its own overflow-x:auto strip; trimmed padding/title sizes; .section-bar/.tabs wrap; player/team header grids stack to one column; .boxscore-headline wraps. @media (max-width: 420px) — hide header search box; .cards to two columns.

risk_posture: CSS-only change; no templates or Python touched.
The change is purely additive CSS gated behind `max-width` media queries, so desktop rendering is untouched.

validation_claims.claim: I verified the stylesheet is well-formed (balanced braces, valid media-query nesting) and reasoned through the flex/order layout by hand.
validation_claims.check_method: Lint/parse almanac.css and render the almanac on a narrow viewport

non_actions.item: Running the live app / screenshotting on mobile
non_actions.reason: I could not run the live app in this sandbox — `flask` isn't installed and there's no seeded DB (both environmental, per CLAUDE.md), and no headless browser was available to screenshot.

section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Make the almanac responsive on mobile
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Date completed 2026-06-06; Branch claude/almanac-mobile-responsive-HIvD1. | Symptom detail: the nav reads "CHEDULE LEADERS CAREER…" on one scroll position and "O27 ALMANAC" on another.
parser_notes: Short, clean AAR. No delegation events or residual-risk statements present.
